categories.title: Prompt Engineering Theory
categories.summary: Foundations of CFG, negative prompts, cross-attention control, weighting syntax, LLM-based prompt rewriting, and survey-paper grounding; the user's prompt is never what the model was trained on.

angles.title: Classifier-free guidance, CFG scaling, negative prompts
Prompt weighting and parser syntax (A1111, Compel, ComfyUI, MJ)
Survey papers on prompt engineering for T2I

categories.title: Image Generation Models
categories.summary: Architectural landscape from diffusion foundations through DiT/MM-DiT and rectified flow to autoregressive transformers, plus Imagen-family technical reports.

angles.title: Autoregressive transformer T2I (Parti, Muse, VAR, gpt-image-1)
Flow matching and rectified flow (SD3, Flux, SANA)

categories.title: Evaluation Metrics
categories.summary: CLIP alignment, FID/CMMD/FD-DINOv2 perceptual, human-preference models (HPSv3, PickScore, ImageReward, MPS), compositional benchmarks, and asset-specific evaluation; no single metric suffices.

angles.title: FID, CMMD, FD-DINOv2 and perceptual metrics
Asset-specific evaluation (text rendering, alpha, logos, icons)

categories.title: Gemini / Imagen Prompting
categories.summary: Google's two stacks (Imagen T2I, Gemini multimodal edit), the checkerboard transparency failure as architectural limit, quirks, and Vertex SDK integration details.

angles.title: The transparent-background checkerboard problem on Google stacks
Documented quirks and artifacts in Imagen/Gemini output

categories.title: OpenAI DALL·E / gpt-image
categories.summary: DALL·E 3 recaptioning and deprecation, gpt-image-1/1.5 native RGBA + input_fidelity, system style behavior, failure modes and moderation stack, cookbook workflows, and production integrations.

angles.title: DALL·E 3 prompt guide and rewriter behavior
Logo and icon generation with gpt-image-1
Failure modes: text rendering and moderation

categories.title: Stable Diffusion / Flux
categories.summary: SD 1.5/2.1/XL fundamentals, Flux family prompting contracts, ControlNet + IP-Adapter + PuLID, LoRA training for brand style, and local pipelines (Diffusers, ComfyUI).

categories.title: Midjourney / Ideogram / Recraft
categories.summary: Commercial closed-source T2I specialists: Midjourney for aesthetic/concept (no API), Ideogram for in-image text, Recraft for native vector SVG and brand styles.

angles.title: Midjourney v6/v7 prompting and style codes
Recraft V3 native vector and brand styles

categories.title: Logo Generation
categories.summary: Logo design theory, prompt patterns by style, text rendering hardness, monograms and palette control, and the SVG vector logo pipeline — a routing + post-processing problem.

angles.title: Logo design theory and brand fundamentals

categories.title: App Icon Generation
categories.summary: iOS HIG specs, Android adaptive + themed icons, PWA manifest + maskable, icon-generation tools survey, and prompt patterns — one 1024² master fans out to every platform variant.

angles.title: iOS app icon HIG specs and Icon Composer
PWA web app manifest and maskable icons

categories.title: UI Illustrations & Graphics
categories.summary: Empty-state/onboarding illustrations, hero images, spot illustrations and icon packs, 3D/isometric/clay/glass, and the illustration-to-production pipeline — consistency is the primary problem.

categories.title: Favicon & Web Assets
categories.summary: Modern favicon spec, OG/Twitter card dynamic images (Satori + resvg), adjacent web assets (email/chat/README), OSS favicon generators, and performance/SEO/accessibility.

angles.title: Adjacent web assets: email, chat unfurlers, README
Performance, SEO, accessibility for web assets

categories.title: Vector / SVG Generation
categories.summary: Native vector generation papers (StarVector, SVGDreamer, Chat2SVG), raster-to-SVG tracers (vtracer, potrace), SVG spec features, LLM-authored SVG, and hybrid vector pipelines.

categories.title: Transparent Backgrounds
categories.summary: The #1 pain: RGBA architecture and LayerDiffuse, difference matting, the checkerboard-drawn-as-pixels failure, matting models (BiRefNet, BRIA RMBG, U²-Net), and end-to-end transparent pipelines.

angles.title: Matting models: BiRefNet, BRIA RMBG, U²-Net

categories.title: Negative Prompting & Artifacts
categories.summary: Negative prompts for assets, artifact taxonomy, regenerate-vs-repair strategies, automated quality scoring (ML + VLM), and deterministic validation checks — a multi-stage QA loop.

categories.title: Style Consistency & Brand
categories.summary: Consistent character/mascot, style transfer via --sref / B-LoRA, brand color palette enforcement, machine-readable brand bundles (DTCG, brand.md, AdCP), and full asset-set consistency.

categories.title: Background Removal & Vectorization
categories.summary: rembg ecosystem (BiRefNet, ISNet, U²-Net), SAM family segmentation, vectorization tooling (vtracer, potrace), commercial BG-removal APIs, and the matte → refine → optimize → export pipeline.

Deployment patterns for upscaling (WebGPU, CoreML, serverless)

categories.title: Open-Source Repos Landscape
categories.summary: OSS prompt-enhancement repos, full-stack asset generators, image-gen SDK wrappers (Vercel AI SDK, OpenRouter), ComfyUI workflow ecosystem, and agent-native webapp gap analysis.

angles.title: Image-gen SDK wrappers (Vercel AI SDK, OpenRouter)

key_findings.finding: Transparent-background generation from Imagen 3/4 and Gemini 2.5/3 Flash Image returns a checkerboard pattern rendered as RGB pixels, not real alpha. This is an architectural limit of RGB-only VAEs plus training-data contamination and cannot be fixed by any prompt phrasing. Fix by routing to gpt-image-1 (background: 'transparent'), Ideogram 3 Turbo (style: 'transparent'), or LayerDiffuse-enabled SDXL/Flux, or by post-process matting with BiRefNet/BRIA RMBG-2.0.

Flux models reject `negative_prompt` outright (API error); Imagen and gpt-image-1 silently ignore it. The only universally-portable 'negative' is positive anchors ('pure white background' instead of 'no checkerboard'). Negative prompts remain valid on SD 1.5 / SDXL via CFG.

No raster diffusion model emits native SVG. The three real paths are: (1) LLM-authored SVG (Claude/GPT) for simple geometric marks, (2) Recraft V3 native-vector API, (3) raster + BiRefNet matte + K-means + vtracer/potrace + SVGO. StarVector/SVGDreamer/Chat2SVG are not production-ready.

Ideogram 3 and gpt-image-1 are the two best in-image text renderers in 2026; both spell reliably up to ~30–50 characters. All other models drop below useful at ~3 words. For brand wordmarks, do not render text in the diffusion sampler — generate a text-free mark and composite real SVG/Canvas typography in the application layer.

Every major provider inserts an invisible LLM prompt rewriter (DALL·E 3, Imagen < 30 words, gpt-image-1 multi-layer, Ideogram 'Magic Prompt'). The plugin must rewrite the user's prompt first so the provider's hidden rewriter has less surface to mangle, and must know the target model's dialect (tag-salad for SD, prose for Flux/Imagen/gpt-image-1).

Every framework-native icon generator converges on one 1024×1024 RGBA PNG as the input contract. The agent's job is to guarantee this master, with the subject inside the inner 66% safe zone; all platform fan-out (iOS squircle, Android adaptive layers, PWA maskable, visionOS parallax, favicons, .ico/.icns) is deterministic CPU work from that single artifact.

Android adaptive icons reserve the outer 18 dp of the 108 dp canvas for system parallax + mask clipping; only the inner 72 dp (~66%) is safe. Most AI outputs fill the whole canvas and get clipped on Pixel launchers. Validation must check tight-bbox of the foreground layer before accepting.

iOS App Store Connect rejects the 1024 marketing icon if it contains an alpha channel. Every pipeline must emit an icon-opaque.png variant pre-flattened onto a user-picked background color in addition to the RGBA master.

sharp (libvips) is ~27× faster than jimp and ~7.7× faster than ImageMagick on resize-crop-sharpen pipelines, and ships prebuilt libvips binaries — no node-gyp or system installs. @resvg/resvg-js is the correct SVG rasterizer (deterministic cross-OS, no system fonts) rather than sharp's built-in librsvg path.

Content-addressed storage + prompt-hash caching is the single highest-leverage engineering investment for a hosted asset pipeline. SHA-256 over a canonicalized (prompt, model, aspect_ratio, seed, params) tuple collapses 20–40% of consumer traffic into one model call. At 1M assets/mo × 25% hit rate × $0.02 blended cost ≈ $5,000/mo saved. Using the hash as BullMQ jobId also deduplicates concurrent identical requests for free.

Brand consistency across an asset set is a 'brand bundle' injection problem. Store DTCG design tokens + style reference images + LoRA/--sref/style_id handle + do_not[] list + canonical logo-mark.svg in a machine-readable bundle (brand.md / AdCP brand.json) and inject at every generation. Validate palette adherence with K-means in LAB + ΔE2000 on each output.

Upscaler choice is asset-type-dependent, not image-dependent. DAT2 fine-tunes (4x-UltraSharpV2, 4xNomos2_hq_dat2, 4x-IllustrationJaNai_V1_DAT2) are the 2026 generalist default. Never run SUPIR or Magnific creative mode on a flat logo — they hallucinate paper texture and gradient banding. For truly flat assets, vectorize-then-rasterize beats raster upscaling.

A VLM-as-judge pass (Claude Sonnet or GPT-4o-mini vision at ~$0.003/call) against 5–7 hard criteria catches ~70% of asset failures and feeds a diff back into the regenerate loop. This is the highest-ROI step in the pipeline and should run after every generation.

MCP is the only true lingua franca for execution across Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Codex, Gemini CLI, and VS Code Copilot. Target MCP spec 2025-06-18 minimum (stable); HTTP+SSE is deprecated. One TypeScript MCP binary + stdio transport serves every host, with Streamable HTTP + OAuth 2.1 + PKCE + RFC 9728 for hosted multi-tenant offerings.

A single SSOT (one SKILL.md per intent + one un-frontmattered rules body) plus a ~60-line sync script (scripts/sync-mirrors.sh) plus a byte-identity CI test (tests/verify_repo.py) is sufficient to satisfy all six coding-agent surfaces (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Codex, Gemini CLI) plus two Copilot surfaces. Humazier is the on-disk reference.

The open-source landscape has mature execution (ComfyUI + LayerDiffuse), SDK abstraction (Vercel AI SDK v5 generateImage), post-processing (rembg/BRIA/BiRefNet, vtracer, pwa-asset-generator, capacitor-assets), platform shape (website + hosted MCP + cross-IDE skills), and brand-bundle formats (brand.md, brandspec, AdCP) — but nothing stitches them into an asset-correctness-first, research-grounded, multi-model, agent-parity product. That is the whitespace.

Cloudflare R2 + Cloudflare CDN is a ~25× cost lever over S3 + CloudFront at scale. 1 M assets/mo × 8 MB × 50 views ≈ 300 TB egress costs ~$25,400/mo on CloudFront and $0 on R2 (egress-free). Combined with content-addressed keys, this delivers the reliability budget the rest of the pipeline needs.

OG images should be rendered via Satori + @resvg/resvg-js + @vercel/og (deterministic, template-based, no T2I hallucination of typography), not via diffusion. Twitter / LinkedIn / Slack / Discord unfurlers flatten alpha and cache aggressively; absolute HTTPS URLs and cache-busting are non-optional.

recommended_tool_surface.name: asset_enhance_prompt
recommended_tool_surface.description: Classify user intent into a closed asset_type enum, select target model by capability (transparency/vector/text-length/license), rewrite prompt in the target model's dialect (tag-salad for SD vs prose for Flux/Imagen/gpt-image-1), return a structured AssetSpec with {positive, negative, weights, aspect_ratio, model_family, post_processing[]}.

recommended_tool_surface.name: asset_generate_logo
recommended_tool_surface.description: Generate a logo asset end-to-end: route to Recraft V3 (native SVG) for vector requests, Ideogram 3 / gpt-image-1 for text-bearing marks, or SDXL + LoRA for brand-locked styles; run BiRefNet matte; vectorize; validate ≤200 paths; return {svg_path, raster_url, master_png_url, mono_variant}.

recommended_tool_surface.name: asset_generate_app_icon
recommended_tool_surface.description: Generate a single 1024×1024 RGBA master (subject inside 676×676 safe zone) and fan out deterministically to iOS AppIcon.appiconset (incl. opaque marketing 1024), Android adaptive (foreground + background + monochrome for Android 13) + play-store 512, PWA 192/512/512-maskable, visionOS 3-layer parallax, favicon bundle, .icns, .ico. Validate safe zone, alpha, App Store opaque requirement.

recommended_tool_surface.name: asset_generate_favicon
recommended_tool_surface.description: Emit a minimal modern favicon bundle: favicon.ico (16/32/48), icon.svg with dark/light prefers-color-scheme, apple-touch-icon.png (180×180 opaque), 192/512 + 512-maskable manifest icons, plus the exact HTML <head> snippet. Reuse brand mark SVG; rasterize per size independently for correct anti-aliasing at each slot.

recommended_tool_surface.name: asset_generate_og_image
recommended_tool_surface.description: Render a 1200×630 Open Graph image via Satori + @resvg/resvg-js (not diffusion) from a JSON template (headline, subhead, brand bundle, optional generated illustration). Deterministic typography, no hallucinated text. Emit Twitter summary_large_image variant simultaneously.

recommended_tool_surface.name: asset_generate_illustration
recommended_tool_surface.description: Generate brand-locked UI illustration (empty state, onboarding, hero) with consistent style across a set. Inject brand bundle via IP-Adapter / --sref / Recraft style_id / Together FLUX.2 brand refs. Validate palette ΔE2000 ≤ 10 on each output; regenerate on drift.

recommended_tool_surface.name: asset_remove_background
recommended_tool_surface.description: Alpha-aware background removal. Route by mode: native (gpt-image-1 background:transparent, Ideogram 3 Turbo style:transparent), layerdiffuse (in-loop RGBA on SDXL/Flux), birefnet (default post-process), rmbg (BRIA RMBG-2.0 hosted for commercial), difference (matte via white-vs-black pair), sam (interactive). Validate no checkerboard pattern and alpha coverage.

recommended_tool_surface.name: asset_vectorize
recommended_tool_surface.description: Raster → SVG. Route by mode: vtracer (full-color), potrace (1-bit), recraft (hosted native vector). Constrain max_paths and palette_size. Post-process with SVGO (conservative preset, preserve viewBox, strip metadata). Return {svg, path_count, size_bytes}.

recommended_tool_surface.name: asset_upscale_refine
recommended_tool_surface.description: Asset-type-aware refinement. Vectorize-then-rasterize for logos/icons; DAT2 fine-tunes (4x-UltraSharpV2, 4x-IllustrationJaNai-DAT2) for flat illustrations; Real-HAT-sharper + CCSR v2 for photos; Flux img2img denoise 0.12–0.20 for polish only. Never SUPIR / Magnific creative on brand assets. Split alpha before RGB upscale, Lanczos upscale alpha, merge.

recommended_tool_surface.name: asset_validate
recommended_tool_surface.description: Tier-0 deterministic validators (dimensions, alpha present/absent, checkerboard FFT signature, safe-zone bbox, file-size budget, JPEG-in-PNG detection) + Tier-1 metrics (VQAScore, OCR Levenshtein vs intended wordmark, K-means + ΔE2000 palette check, 16² contrast) + optional Tier-2 VLM-as-judge rubric. Return structured violations + suggested repair prompt.

recommended_tool_surface.name: asset_brand_bundle_parse
recommended_tool_surface.description: Ingest brand.md, brandspec.dev, AdCP brand.json, or raw DTCG tokens file; auto-configure canonical brand bundle (palette hex, typography, style reference image paths, LoRA/--sref/style_id handles, do_not[] list, canonical logo-mark.svg). Supports URL ingestion (dembrandt-style) for on-the-fly extraction from a live site.

recommended_tool_surface.name: asset_export_icon_set
recommended_tool_surface.description: Standalone OSS replacement for appicon.co: given a 1024×1024 RGBA source, emit the full cross-platform ZIP (iOS AppIcon.appiconset/Contents.json, Android mipmap-* + adaptive XML + monochrome, PWA manifest icons + maskable, .icns, .ico, favicon bundle) with deterministic CPU work via sharp + @capacitor/assets + icon-gen + custom emitters. Gateway-drug tool; runs in browser via jSquash + JSZip for zero-upload UX.
